acceptance_targets: [ch13-prose-001] The revised chapter contains no more than three instances of 'the way' introducing a simile or comparison clause. The remaining comparisons use at least two different syntactic forms. Read the six target passages in sequence — they should no longer produce the sensation of a repeated template.

[ch13-prose-002] The revised chapter contains no more than two instances of 'Not X. Not Y.' anaphoric negation. The feeling described at line 95 is rendered through at least one physical sensation or concrete image rather than purely through negation.

[ch13-prose-003] The word 'specific' (as an intensifier-adjective modifying a noun phrase) appears no more than twice in the revised chapter. At least one of the reworked passages conveys precision through concrete sensory detail rather than through the word 'specific' itself.

[ch13-prose-004] The revised chapter contains no more than two 'which was [evaluative commentary]' constructions. The bathroom description at line 85 conveys humiliation through sensory detail or Dan's physical response rather than through an evaluative label.

[ch13-craft-001] The revised passage at lines 147-165 does not describe direct sunlight entering through the kitchen window in the evening. Any directional light described is consistent with the east-facing kitchen window established in the continuity sheet. The temporal-visual clock (light narrowing, vanishing) is preserved through a spatially consistent light source.

[ch13-craft-002] The revised passage at lines 105-108 does not enumerate more than three items after 'Which was everything.' The revised pretzel cart passage at line 123 contains at least two sensory details specific to the cart/vendor and does not use the phrases 'optimization framework,' 'human-scaled,' or 'entirely legible.' The thematic contrast between the cart and the proxy world is communicated through what Dan perceives, not through analytical commentary.

[ch13-craft-003] The revised text from line 105 to the end of the chapter contains at least two moments where dark comedy coexists with Dan's emotional devastation — beats where the absurdity of his situation is legible alongside the pain, rather than the pain having fully displaced the humor. At least one of these beats occurs after line 131 (the homecoming). The final image of the chapter retains its quiet devastation.

findings.acceptance_test: The revised chapter contains no more than three instances of 'the way' introducing a simile or comparison clause. The remaining comparisons use at least two different syntactic forms. Read the six target passages in sequence — they should no longer produce the sensation of a repeated template.
findings.evidence: Six instances of 'the way [comparison clause]' as simile connector: (1) snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:5 'Not the way Mira would do it'; (2) snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:39 'the way throwing a rock through a window must feel'; (3) snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:63 'the way you'd correct a smudge on a lens'; (4) snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:73 'the way you'd move a chair to avoid a puddle'; (5) snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:105 'the way a river adjusts around a stone in the streambed'; (6) snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:119 'the way an actor in a play about offices would carry one as a prop'.
findings.problem: The 'the way [comparison]' construction is the chapter's dominant simile vehicle, appearing six times. By the third instance the reader can predict the syntactic shape before the content arrives. The construction flattens what are otherwise strong individual metaphors by housing them in identical frames, producing a rhythmic monotone where variety is needed. Once is a choice; six times is autopilot.
findings.rewrite_direction: Retain 2-3 of the strongest 'the way' similes — the river/stone at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:105 and the rock-through-window at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:39 are the most earned. For the remaining 3-4 instances, vary the connective syntax: use direct metaphor without connector ('His briefcase was a prop, an actor's briefcase'), subordinate clause ('as if correcting a smudge'), apposition, or eliminate the comparison entirely where the literal description is strong enough on its own. The smudge-on-a-lens at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:63 and the chair-to-avoid-puddle at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:73 are the weakest and should be reworked first.

findings.acceptance_test: The revised chapter contains no more than two instances of 'Not X. Not Y.' anaphoric negation. The feeling described at line 95 is rendered through at least one physical sensation or concrete image rather than purely through negation.
findings.evidence: Five instances of 'Not X. Not Y.' anaphoric negation: (1) snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:5 'Not strategically. Not the way Mira would do it'; (2) snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:9 'Not passively bad, not his usual ambient incompetence'; (3) snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:63 'Not censored. Not punished.'; (4) snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:95 'not anxiety, not anger, something lower and quieter'; (5) snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:105 'Not by removing the stone. Not by acknowledging the stone's objection to being in the river.'
findings.problem: The 'Not X. Not Y.' construction is used to narrow meaning through elimination. It is effective in the opening (line 5) where it establishes Dan's mental register and effective at the climax (line 105) where it renders the system's indifference. But at five appearances the rhetorical move becomes predictable — the reader knows that when Dan encounters something he can't name, two negatives will precede the positive. The pattern signals that the prose has a default move for handling ambiguity.
findings.rewrite_direction: Preserve the anaphoric negation at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:5 (opening, sets voice) and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:105 (thematic climax, earned by placement). Rework the remaining three instances. At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:9, let Dan's intention arrive directly without the negation preamble. At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:63, try a single-sentence formulation that captures the correction's casualness without the anaphoric structure. At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:95, name the feeling through sensory or physical detail rather than defining it by what it isn't.

findings.acceptance_test: The word 'specific' (as an intensifier-adjective modifying a noun phrase) appears no more than twice in the revised chapter. At least one of the reworked passages conveys precision through concrete sensory detail rather than through the word 'specific' itself.
findings.evidence: Four instances of 'the specific [noun/adjective] of [extended noun phrase]': (1) snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:5 'A specific, physical, unmistakably human problem'; (2) snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:69 'the specific musculature of a person who used his body for work that couldn't be proxied'; (3) snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:95 'the specific, humiliating hopefulness of a child misbehaving for attention'; (4) snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:111 'the specific numbness of having sat too long on a hard surface without shifting his weight'.
findings.problem: The word 'specific' is used as an intensifier-adjective four times to signal that the narrator is rendering something precisely. The construction becomes self-undermining: by repeatedly announcing specificity, the prose advertises its own technique rather than simply being specific. The word does real work at line 69 (the bike messenger's calves) where the physical detail earns it. Elsewhere it functions as a verbal tic that says 'pay attention, this detail matters' when the detail should announce its own importance through selection and placement.
findings.rewrite_direction: Retain 'specific' at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:69 where the physical detail earns the intensifier. At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:5, cut 'specific' — 'a physical, unmistakably human problem' is stronger without it. At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:95, replace with the sensory detail itself — show the hopefulness through Dan's body rather than labeling it. At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:111, 'the numbness of having sat too long' is sufficient; 'specific' adds nothing the reader can't already feel.

findings.acceptance_test: The revised chapter contains no more than two 'which was [evaluative commentary]' constructions. The bathroom description at line 85 conveys humiliation through sensory detail or Dan's physical response rather than through an evaluative label.
findings.evidence: Four instances of 'which was [evaluative commentary]' as parenthetical gloss: (1) snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:7 'which was the worst part'; (2) snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:17 'the building optimized for servers rather than bodies, which of course it was'; (3) snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:85 'which was its own kind of humiliation'; (4) snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:107 'Which was everything.'
findings.problem: The 'which was' parenthetical functions as a narrator's aside — a small editorial gloss appended to a description. Used once or twice, it's a natural feature of close-third narration where the character's evaluative voice overlaps with the narrator's. At four instances, it becomes a mannerism: the prose describes something, then appends a 'which was [judgment]' tag to make sure the reader caught the significance. The pattern is trust-eroding — it suggests the prose doesn't trust its own imagery to land without commentary.
findings.rewrite_direction: Retain the strongest instance — snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:107 'Which was everything' works because of its sentence-fragment isolation and its position as the thematic pivot. For the other three: at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:7, fold the evaluation into the description rather than appending it ('not the volume of it but the practiced efficiency of a nine-year-old who knew when to withdraw — Theo's headphones going on before the voices even peaked'). At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:17, cut 'which of course it was' entirely; the reader already understands. At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:85, rework so the humiliation is rendered rather than labeled.

findings.acceptance_test: The revised passage at lines 147-165 does not describe direct sunlight entering through the kitchen window in the evening. Any directional light described is consistent with the east-facing kitchen window established in the continuity sheet. The temporal-visual clock (light narrowing, vanishing) is preserved through a spatially consistent light source.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:147 — 'The evening light came through the kitchen window and laid a long rectangle across the hallway floor, almost reaching his feet. He watched it shorten as the sun moved behind the brownstone across the street.' The continuity sheet states under environmental_constants: 'The apartment faces east; morning light comes through the kitchen window and hits the table where Theo eats breakfast.' An east-facing kitchen window receives morning light; it cannot receive evening light from the west.
findings.problem: The chapter shows evening light entering through the kitchen window, but the continuity sheet establishes that the kitchen window faces east (receives morning light). Evening sun comes from the west and cannot enter an east-facing window. This is a spatial impossibility given the established apartment orientation. The detail is load-bearing — it provides the chapter's visual clock (the light narrowing and vanishing as evening progresses) across lines 147-165.
findings.rewrite_direction: Two options for snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:147: (A) Move the light source to a west-facing window — the hallway or living room window could face west or south, allowing evening light. Dan could observe light coming through a different window from his position on the couch. (B) Replace the evening light with a different temporal marker — the quality of ambient light diminishing, streetlights activating, the shift from daylight to lamplight in the apartment. If using option A, the language at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:163 ('The rectangle of light narrowed to a bright line, then vanished') should track the revised light source consistently.

findings.acceptance_test: The revised passage at lines 105-108 does not enumerate more than three items after 'Which was everything.' The revised pretzel cart passage at line 123 contains at least two sensory details specific to the cart/vendor and does not use the phrases 'optimization framework,' 'human-scaled,' or 'entirely legible.' The thematic contrast between the cart and the proxy world is communicated through what Dan perceives, not through analytical commentary.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:105-108 — 'He was weather. A warm front that had drifted through the building and would drift out again, producing no measurable effect on anything the system cared about. Which was everything. Which was the documents and the schedules and the optimization metrics and the resource alignments and the bilateral sensitivity parameters and whatever it was they were fighting about with Gemini-state — all of it, the entire enormous humming architecture of machine governance that Dan had spent the day trying to disrupt with his wrong signatures and his profanity and his extended lunch and his three-hour sit on a toilet he didn't need to use.' Also snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:123 — 'The pretzel cart was a thing that existed independently of any optimization framework. A man with a cart and a heating element and a purpose that was entirely legible: he made pretzels. People bought them. The transaction was human-scaled, complete, and visible from start to finish.'
findings.problem: Both passages drift from close-third narration into essayistic register. At lines 105-108, the 'weather' metaphor is earned, but the subsequent enumeration ('the documents and the schedules and the optimization metrics and the resource alignments and the bilateral sensitivity parameters') reads as a thesis statement summarizing the chapter's argument rather than Dan's thought in the moment. At line 123, 'a thing that existed independently of any optimization framework' and 'The transaction was human-scaled, complete, and visible from start to finish' use analytical language ('optimization framework,' 'human-scaled,' 'transaction') that sounds like the narrator's gloss rather than Dan's interior voice. Dan's lexical signature per the style bible is concrete and bodily when honest — he would notice the pretzel smell, the man's hands, the cash exchange, not frame it as a thesis about legible purpose.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:105-108, trim the enumeration. The 'weather' metaphor and 'Which was everything' are strong enough to carry the meaning — the catalogue that follows over-explains. Cut or compress the list from 'Which was the documents...' through '...three-hour sit on a toilet he didn't need to use' to 2-3 concrete items that stay in Dan's physical experience of the day rather than abstracting it. At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:123, anchor the pretzel cart observation in Dan's body and perception: what he sees (the man's hands, the cash drawer, the napkin dispenser), what he smells, what the simplicity feels like in his chest — rather than framing it as a proposition about optimization frameworks. Let the contrast emerge from the sensory detail, not from the narrator's analysis.

findings.acceptance_test: The revised text from line 105 to the end of the chapter contains at least two moments where dark comedy coexists with Dan's emotional devastation — beats where the absurdity of his situation is legible alongside the pain, rather than the pain having fully displaced the humor. At least one of these beats occurs after line 131 (the homecoming). The final image of the chapter retains its quiet devastation.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:105-165 — From the 'weather' metaphor through the end of the chapter, the comic register that drives the first two-thirds of the chapter (the Robert Paulson signature, the profanity replacement, the 'personal maintenance time' notation) disappears entirely. The final sixty lines are straight devastation with no dark comedy. The chapter spine designates texture_mode as 'comic.' The style bible's register_commitment states: 'The comedy must not protect them from the humiliation; the humiliation must not neutralize the comedy. Render both registers at full force, often in the same scene.'
findings.problem: The chapter's comic register peaks at line 103 ('Personal maintenance time') and then vanishes. The homecoming sequence (lines 131-165) is emotionally powerful but tonally uniform — quiet devastation without any of the dark-comic texture that distinguishes this novel's mode from conventional literary realism. The pretzel cart, the empty briefcase, the subway ads, the walk home — these are all opportunities for the kind of comedy-inside-despair that the chapter executes brilliantly in its first half (the system replacing profanity with compliance, the coffee timed to the elevator). The style bible warns against 'darkening the humiliation into dystopian grimness.' The final third tips into that territory.
findings.rewrite_direction: The devastation of the ending is correct and should be preserved — Dan sitting in the dark, not telling Mira, is the right emotional destination. But the path there should maintain the novel's comic DNA. At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:119-127, the empty briefcase and subway ads are rendered solemnly; find the bitter comedy in these details (the briefcase as prop, the streaming service ad for 'choosing' — these premises are inherently absurd and the prose should let that absurdity register even as Dan's misery deepens). At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:141-143, Dan's imagined speech to Mira is already halfway to dark comedy ('I'm furniture, Mira. I'm a chair that showed up in the wrong room'); let a beat of that dark humor land rather than immediately undercutting it with 'Not brave. Not angry. Not even sad.' The comic texture can coexist with the devastation — the chapter proves this in its first half.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's risk zone is the gap between dignity and absurdity. The characters are adults with professional histories, a child, a mortgage — people with legitimate self-respect — who have been reduced to warm bodies in an optimization framework they cannot understand. The comedy must not protect them from the humiliation; the humiliation must not neutralize the comedy. Render both registers at full force, often in the same scene. The temptation will be to soften the comedy into gentle satire or to darken the humiliation into dystopian grimness — resist both. The novel's territory is the painful and the funny coexisting without one defusing the other. Commit to the specific physical embarrassment of being professionally irrelevant. Commit to the specific tenderness of parenting under surveillance. Commit to the specific grief of a marriage that might still be alive inside a system that has classified it as infrastructure. When in doubt, choose the version that makes you wince and then laugh, or laugh and then wince — both are correct.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism is a craft failure equivalent to a plot hole. When a scene requires the reader to sit with discomfort — economic shame, marital cruelty, parental failure, institutional humiliation, the specific indignity of being professionally redundant — render it with full specificity. 'They struggled with the situation' is not a sentence that belongs in this novel. Name the thing. Show the body. Let the reader be uncomfortable.
aesthetic_risk_policy.intimacy_and_desire_policy: Dan and Mira's residual physical attraction is a narrative force, not background radiation. When the novel's relational pressure calls for it, render desire with the same specificity as any other emotional state — where in the body it lives, how it changes the air in a room, the precise quality of proximity when two people who know each other's bodies are trying to pretend they don't. The almost-kiss in Chapter 7 should be as fully rendered as any confrontation scene.
aesthetic_risk_policy.register_commitment: This is a dark comedy, not a cautionary tale, not a dystopia, not a satire with a thesis. The novel should not argue about AI. It should render what it feels like to be a body in a world where bodies are overhead. The comic register is not a veneer over a serious message — it IS the message. A scene that is genuinely funny about genuine suffering is doing more literary work than a scene that is solemnly observant about the same material.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the default for all spoken English in this novel. Uncontracted forms should be reserved for deliberate emphasis, Lena Morse's institutional register, or moments where a character is being carefully precise. If a line of dialogue uses 'do not' instead of 'don't' or 'I am' instead of 'I'm,' there should be a reason audible in the scene's pressure.
dialogue_rules.interruption_and_overlap_policy: Use interruptions, sentence fragments, and crosstalk when scene pressure warrants. Dan and Mira in particular should interrupt each other — they know each other's arguments before the sentences end. But every interruption must be motivated; do not scatter dashes for rhythmic texture alone.
dialogue_rules.leverage_shift_guidance: Every exchange of more than three lines should shift the power balance between speakers. This doesn't require overt conflict — a silence, a deflection, a piece of information withheld or accidentally revealed, a joke that lands wrong, a question left unanswered can all shift leverage. The dialogue must be going somewhere, not circling.
dialogue_rules.silence_as_dialogue: Silence, non-answers, and topic changes are valid dialogue moves and often more revealing than speech. When a character doesn't respond to something, the reader should feel the weight of what wasn't said. Render these moments through physical action or observed stillness, not by narrating that the character chose not to speak.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should share the same structural rhythm. Vary: scene count (single-scene compression vs. multi-scene chapters), dialogue density (some chapters are conversation-heavy, others are nearly silent), interiority depth (some chapters live mostly in the POV character's head, others are externally rendered), pacing (some chapters cover hours, others cover weeks in montage), and tonal register (comic chapters adjacent to devastating ones). The contrast between chapters is itself a narrative tool — use it to create whiplash, irony, and accumulating emotional force.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, precise, and register-sensitive. The prose absorbs the vocabulary of its setting: corporate language, tech terminology, bureaucratic formulas — but always filtered through the POV character's relationship to that language. Dan uses corporate words reflexively and with slight discomfort. Mira uses strategic vocabulary as native speech. The narrator should not editorialize on the language; let the words sit in context and let the reader hear the dissonance. Avoid: prestige-literary abstractions that haven't been earned by sensory grounding, and journalistic explainer-prose that breaks character to tell the reader about the world.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating between Dan and Mira as primary POV characters. The camera stays tight to the current POV character's perceptions and vocabulary — what Dan notices is different from what Mira notices. In shared-POV chapters, the rotation should be clear (section breaks or sustained passages in one POV before shifting) rather than head-hopping within a scene.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and responsive to scene pressure. Short declarative sentences for comic timing, for shock, and for moments of clarity. Longer, more syntactically complex sentences for interiority, sensory immersion, and emotional complexity. The rhythm should accelerate during confrontation and slow during reflection. Avoid settling into any single rhythmic default — if three consecutive paragraphs use the same sentence structure, one of them is wrong.
